title: Frothy Fun on National Cappuccino Day
story: Coffee shops everywhere get extra busy on November 8 for National Cappuccino Day! This creamy Italian drink is made with espresso, steamed milk, and fluffy foam on top. title: Harvey's Wacky Wallbanger Day

title: X-Ray Vision Day for Real!
story: Every November 8, doctors and medical workers around the world celebrate World Radiography Day! activity_answer: They take X-rays and medical pictures

title: The Accidental Discovery of X-Rays
story: A strange glow caught Wilhelm Röntgen's eye in his dark laboratory. The German physicist had stumbled upon a groundbreaking discovery on November 8, 1895. title: A Sci-Fi Star Is Born

activity_question: What famous science fiction author was born on November 8, 1932?
follow_up_question: What was the title of one of Ben Bova's books mentioned in the story?

title: Astronaut Dale Gardner's Stellar Beginning
story: Blast off! Dale A. Gardner rocketed into the world on November 8, 1948. Born in Fairmont, Minnesota, this future space explorer would grow up to become a naval commander and astronaut. Gardner's cosmic adventures included two thrilling shuttle missions: STS-8 and STS-51A. His journey through the stars inspired many earthbound dreamers.
activity_question: What two shuttle missions did Dale A. Gardner participate in?
follow_up_question: What skills do you think an astronaut needs?

title: Olympic Champion Fred Newhouse Sprints into Life
story: Speedy Fred Newhouse raced into the world on November 8, 1948, in Honey Grove, Texas. This future track star would go on to win Olympic gold in the 4×400m relay and silver in the 400m at the 1976 Games. Newhouse's lightning-fast legs and team spirit made him a true champion on the world stage.
activity_question: What Olympic medals did Fred Newhouse win?
activity_choices: Gold in 4×400m relay and silver in 400m
Silver in 4×400m relay and gold in 400m
Bronze in long jump and gold in high jump
activity_answer: Gold in 4×400m relay and silver in 400m
follow_up_question: Can you name another Olympic track and field event?

title: A Storyteller's Journey Begins
story: A master storyteller took his first breath in Nagasaki, Japan. Kazuo Ishiguro, born in 1954 on November 8, would grow up to captivate readers worldwide. His novels like 'Klara and the Sun' and 'The Remains of the Day' blend deep emotions with thought-provoking ideas. Ishiguro's unique Japanese-British perspective enriches his tales, making him a beloved author across cultures.
activity_question: Which of Kazuo Ishiguro's novels is mentioned in the story?
follow_up_question: Can you name another of Ishiguro's books mentioned in the story?

title: Jamaican Music Star's Birthday Surprise
story: Reggae fusion queen Diana King first opened her eyes in St. Catherine, Jamaica on November 8, 1970. Her powerful voice would later charm listeners worldwide with hits like 'Shy Guy' and a soulful remake of 'I Say a Little Prayer'. King's unique blend of reggae, R&B, and pop helped put Jamaican music on the global map.
activity_question: What famous song did Diana King remake?
follow_up_question: Can you name another Jamaican music style besides reggae?

title: Czech Hockey Star Skates onto the Scene
story: Frantisek Kaberle glided into the world on November 8, 1973, in Kladno, Czech Republic. This future ice hockey superstar would grow up to become a skilled defenseman. Kaberle's talent shone brightest when he helped Team Czech win Olympic gold in 1998, bringing glory to his homeland on the international stage.
activity_question: What position did Frantisek Kaberle play in ice hockey?
follow_up_question: Can you name another sport that has defensemen?

title: Oxford's Treasure Trove of Knowledge Opens
story: Books galore! In 1602, a magnificent library burst onto the scene at Oxford University. The Bodleian Library, named after its founder Thomas Bodley, opened its doors on November 8. This wasn't just any library - it was a palace of learning, filled with rare books and ancient manuscripts. Students and scholars flocked to explore its vast collection, eager to unlock the wisdom within its pages.
activity_question: What was the name of the library that opened at Oxford University?
bonus_fact: The Bodleian Library is one of the oldest libraries in Europe and holds over 13 million items.

title: The Birth of Bourbon: America's Whiskey
story: Sip on this: in 1789, a clever man named Elijah Craig had a tasty idea. On November 8, he became the first person to distill bourbon whiskey from corn. This happened in Bourbon County, Virginia, which is now part of Kentucky. Craig's creation would become a beloved American drink, known for its smooth, rich flavor.
activity_question: What was Elijah Craig the first to do?
bonus_fact: Bourbon is now recognized as America's official native spirit by an act of Congress.

title: Zapping Bugs: The Electric Insect Zapper
story: Bzzt! In 1910, William H. Frost had a bright idea to keep pesky bugs away. He invented an electrical device to zap insects, receiving the first U.S. patent for this clever contraption on November 8. Frost's creation paved the way for modern bug zappers, helping people enjoy outdoor activities with fewer buzzing intruders.
activity_question: What did William H. Frost invent?
follow_up_question: Why might people want to use an electrical insect destroyer?
